reactions.name: Carbon dioxide forms carbonic acid
reactions.notes: Contributes to natural acidity of rainwater.

reactions.name: Nitrogen dioxide to nitric acid
reactions.conditions: Nitrogen dioxide reacts with oxygen and water.
reactions.notes: Major acid-rain pathway from nitrogen oxides.

reactions.name: Sulfur dioxide in rainwater
reactions.notes: Explains acid rain from sulfur dioxide.

reactions.name: Sulfur trioxide hydration
reactions.notes: Acid rain and industrial chemistry reaction.

reactions.name: Calcium bicarbonate with hydrochloric acid
reactions.conditions: Calcium bicarbonate reacts with hydrochloric acid.
reactions.notes: Bicarbonates release carbon dioxide with acids.

reactions.name: Sodium bicarbonate with nitric acid
reactions.conditions: Sodium bicarbonate reacts with nitric acid.
reactions.notes: Bicarbonate neutralizes acid and releases carbon dioxide.

reactions.name: Baking powder releases carbon dioxide
reactions.conditions: Baking soda mixed with tartaric acid and heated/moistened.
reactions.observation: Carbon dioxide bubbles raise cakes and bread.
reactions.notes: Core leavening reaction of baking powder.

reactions.name: Calcium carbonate with nitric acid
reactions.conditions: Calcium carbonate reacts with nitric acid.

reactions.name: Sodium carbonate with sulfuric acid
reactions.conditions: Sodium carbonate reacts with dilute sulfuric acid.
reactions.notes: Carbonate salts release carbon dioxide with acids.

reactions.name: Hydration of ethene to ethanol
reactions.conditions: High temperature and pressure with phosphoric acid catalyst.

reactions.name: Hydration of ethyne to ethanal
reactions.conditions: 20% H2SO4 and 2% HgSO4 at about 80 C.
reactions.notes: Mercury catalyst is toxic and discouraged industrially.

reactions.name: Hydrogenation of ethene
reactions.notes: Used in hydrogenation of unsaturated compounds.

reactions.name: Ammonia in water
reactions.observation: Ammonium and hydroxide ions form partially.

reactions.name: Alcoholic fermentation
reactions.notes: Used in bread and alcoholic beverages.

reactions.name: Cellular respiration
reactions.observation: Glucose is oxidized to release usable energy.

reactions.name: Oxidation of ethanol to acetic acid
Photosynthesis
reactions.observation: Carbon dioxide and water become glucose and oxygen.
reactions.notes: Overall simplified equation for oxygenic photosynthesis.

reactions.name: Aluminium hydroxide calcination
reactions.observation: Pure aluminium oxide forms and water is removed.
reactions.notes: Final preparation step for alumina from purified aluminium hydroxide.

reactions.name: Calcination of hydrated iron oxide
reactions.observation: Water is removed from hydrated iron oxide.
reactions.notes: Example of removing moisture from ore.

reactions.name: Calcination of zinc carbonate
reactions.observation: Carbon dioxide is released and zinc oxide remains.
reactions.notes: Used for calamine before zinc extraction.

reactions.name: Carbon reduction of copper oxide
reactions.notes: Reduction method for lower/mid-reactivity metal oxides.

reactions.name: Carbon reduction of lead oxide
reactions.notes: Demonstrates reduction of a metal oxide by carbon.

reactions.name: Carbon reduction of zinc oxide
reactions.observation: Zinc metal and carbon monoxide form.

reactions.name: Reduction of hematite by carbon monoxide
Ammonium hydrogen carbonate formation
reactions.conditions: Pass carbon dioxide into ammoniated brine/water.

reactions.name: Bleaching powder formation
reactions.conditions: Pass chlorine over calcium hydroxide around 40 C.

reactions.name: Oleum formation
reactions.conditions: Absorb sulfur trioxide in concentrated sulfuric acid.
reactions.notes: Used to avoid sulfuric acid mist in contact process.

reactions.name: Slaking quicklime
reactions.observation: Heat is released and slaked lime forms.
reactions.notes: Strongly exothermic hydration of calcium oxide.

reactions.name: Sodium carbonate formation from sodium hydroxide
reactions.conditions: Pass carbon dioxide through sodium hydroxide solution.
reactions.notes: First step in washing soda preparation.

reactions.name: Combustion of methane
reactions.conditions: Methane burns in oxygen or air.
reactions.observation: Carbon dioxide, water vapor, and heat are produced.
reactions.notes: Exothermic reaction; about 890 kJ/mol under standard combustion conditions.

reactions.name: Hydrogen combustion
reactions.conditions: Hydrogen mixed with oxygen and ignited.
reactions.observation: Water forms with release of energy.
reactions.notes: Explosive mixtures require strict safety precautions.

reactions.name: Magnesium combustion
reactions.observation: Bright white flame and white magnesium oxide ash.

reactions.name: Sulfur combustion
reactions.conditions: Burn sulfur in oxygen or air.
reactions.observation: Blue flame and choking sulfur dioxide gas.
reactions.notes: Sulfur dioxide contributes to acid rain after further oxidation.

reactions.name: Nylon 6,6 formation
reactions.conditions: Heat adipic acid with hexamethylenediamine, titanium oxide catalyst.

reactions.name: Ferric hydroxide to rust
reactions.conditions: Moist air after iron oxidation steps.
reactions.notes: Late step in rusting of iron.

reactions.name: Rusting of iron
reactions.conditions: Iron exposed to oxygen and moisture.
reactions.observation: Brown hydrated iron(III) oxide rust develops over time.
reactions.notes: Electrochemical corrosion process simplified as a net reaction.

reactions.name: Copper carbonate decomposition
reactions.observation: Green solid turns black; CO2 evolves.

reactions.name: Hydrogen peroxide decomposition
reactions.conditions: Catalyst such as MnO2 or iodide.

reactions.name: Hypochlorous acid releases nascent oxygen
reactions.observation: Reactive oxygen bleaches colored substances and kills germs.

reactions.name: Urea from ammonium carbamate
reactions.conditions: Industrial urea process after ammonium carbamate formation.

reactions.name: Baking soda formation from ammonium hydrogen carbonate
reactions.conditions: Ammonium hydrogen carbonate reacts with sodium chloride.
reactions.observation: Sodium hydrogen carbonate and ammonium chloride form.

reactions.name: Silver chloride precipitation
reactions.conditions: Aqueous silver nitrate and chloride solution.
reactions.notes: Used as a chloride ion test.

reactions.name: Copper silver galvanic reaction
reactions.observation: Copper oxidizes while silver ions reduce to silver metal.
reactions.notes: Galvanic displacement between copper and silver ions.

reactions.name: Daniell cell overall reaction
reactions.conditions: Zinc and copper half-cells connected through salt bridge.
reactions.observation: Zinc dissolves, copper deposits, and electric current flows.
reactions.notes: Overall reaction for a classic galvanic cell.

reactions.name: Dry cell overall reaction
reactions.conditions: Zinc-carbon dry cell paste with ammonium ions and manganese dioxide.
reactions.observation: Chemical energy converts to electrical energy.

reactions.name: Chlor-alkali electrolysis
reactions.observation: Hydrogen, chlorine, and sodium hydroxide are produced.

reactions.name: Molten sodium chloride electrolysis
reactions.conditions: Molten sodium chloride and electric current.
reactions.observation: Sodium metal forms at cathode and chlorine gas at anode.

reactions.name: Copper electrorefining anode reaction
reactions.conditions: Impure copper anode in copper sulfate solution.
reactions.observation: Copper dissolves from the impure anode.

reactions.name: Copper electrorefining cathode reaction
reactions.conditions: Pure copper cathode in copper sulfate solution.
reactions.observation: Pure copper plates onto the cathode.

reactions.name: Nitric oxide formation from air
reactions.conditions: Very high temperature such as lightning or engines.
reactions.observation: Nitric oxide can oxidize further to nitrogen dioxide.
reactions.notes: Important in smog and acid-rain chemistry.

reactions.name: Ammonium chloride with sodium hydroxide
reactions.conditions: Heat ammonium chloride with sodium hydroxide.

reactions.name: Calcium carbide produces ethyne
reactions.notes: Used to prepare acetylene; unsafe for food ripening.

reactions.name: Laboratory ammonia from ammonium chloride and calcium oxide
reactions.conditions: Heat ammonium chloride with calcium oxide.

reactions.name: Laboratory ammonia from ammonium chloride and slaked lime
reactions.conditions: Heat ammonium chloride with calcium hydroxide.
reactions.notes: Alternative laboratory method of preparing ammonia.

reactions.name: Calcium chloride hydration
reactions.conditions: Calcium chloride binds water during crystallization.

reactions.name: Oleum dilution to sulfuric acid
reactions.notes: Final step for producing liquid sulfuric acid.

reactions.name: Methane from carbon dioxide and hydrogen
Aluminum chloride hydrolysis
reactions.observation: Aluminum hydroxide and hydrochloric acid form.

reactions.name: Bleaching powder reacts with water
reactions.observation: Calcium chloride and hypochlorous acid form.
reactions.notes: Hypochlorous acid then bleaches and disinfects.

reactions.name: Chlorine water formation
reactions.observation: Hydrochloric acid and hypochlorous acid form.

reactions.name: Silicon tetrachloride hydrolysis
reactions.conditions: Moisture or water reacts with silicon tetrachloride.

reactions.name: Sodium aluminate hydrolysis
reactions.conditions: Add water to hot sodium aluminate solution.
reactions.observation: Aluminium hydroxide separates while sodium hydroxide remains in solution.
reactions.notes: Step in preparing purified aluminium hydroxide.

reactions.name: Urea hydrolysis in soil
reactions.observation: Ammonium ions become available to plants.
reactions.notes: Explains nitrogen supply from urea fertilizer.

reactions.name: Sulfuric acid ionization
reactions.observation: Hydrogen ions and sulfate ions form.

reactions.name: Ammonium cyanate to urea
reactions.notes: Historical example linking inorganic and organic chemistry.

reactions.name: Aluminum hydroxide antacid neutralization
reactions.conditions: Aluminum hydroxide reacts with hydrochloric acid.

reactions.name: Calcium oxide with hydrochloric acid
reactions.conditions: Basic oxide reacts with hydrochloric acid.

reactions.name: Copper oxide with sulfuric acid
reactions.conditions: Warm dilute sulfuric acid reacts with copper(II) oxide.

reactions.name: Ethanoic acid neutralizes sodium hydroxide
reactions.conditions: Aqueous ethanoic acid and sodium hydroxide.
reactions.notes: Shows weak acid behavior of fatty acids.

reactions.name: Magnesium hydroxide antacid neutralization
reactions.conditions: Magnesium hydroxide reacts with stomach acid.

reactions.name: Neutralization net ionic equation
reactions.conditions: Acid and base mixed in aqueous solution.
reactions.observation: Water forms and heat may be released.
reactions.notes: Core ionic equation for strong acid-strong base neutralization.

reactions.name: Sodium lauryl sulfate formation
reactions.conditions: Neutralize lauryl hydrogen sulfate with sodium hydroxide.

reactions.name: Strong acid-base neutralization
reactions.observation: Heat may be released; salt and water form.
reactions.notes: Net ionic form is H+ + OH- -> H2O.

reactions.name: Sulfuric acid neutralizes calcium hydroxide
reactions.conditions: Dilute sulfuric acid with calcium hydroxide.
reactions.observation: Calcium sulfate salt and water form.
reactions.notes: Shows acidic property of sulfuric acid.

reactions.name: Sulfuric acid with sodium hydroxide
reactions.conditions: Mix sulfuric acid and sodium hydroxide in correct ratio.

reactions.name: Bauxite purification with sodium hydroxide
reactions.observation: Hydrated aluminium oxide converts into soluble sodium aluminate.
reactions.notes: Used in chemical purification of bauxite.

reactions.name: Oxidation of ethanal to ethanoic acid
reactions.notes: Preparation of fatty/organic acid from aldehyde.

reactions.name: Oxidation of ethene to ethylene glycol
reactions.notes: Test for unsaturation and preparation of glycol.

reactions.name: Sulfur dioxide oxidation
reactions.conditions: Atmospheric oxygen, catalysts, or contact-process conditions.
reactions.notes: Step toward sulfuric acid formation in acid rain.

reactions.name: Daniell cell zinc oxidation
reactions.observation: Zinc metal enters solution as zinc ions.
reactions.notes: Anode half-reaction of the Daniell cell.

reactions.name: Silver electroplating anode reaction
reactions.conditions: Silver anode in silver nitrate solution.

reactions.name: Water electrolysis anode reaction
reactions.conditions: Anode during electrolysis of water/alkaline solution.

reactions.name: Photosynthesis detailed equation
reactions.observation: Plants store light energy as glucose.
reactions.notes: Detailed form often used to show water as both reactant and product.

reactions.name: Propene polymerization
reactions.conditions: Titanium chloride catalyst, about 140 atm and 120 C.

reactions.name: Vinyl chloride polymerization
reactions.conditions: High pressure and temperature with organic peroxide.

reactions.name: Aluminum hydroxide precipitation from nitrate
reactions.conditions: Aluminum nitrate solution with sodium hydroxide.
reactions.observation: White gelatinous aluminum hydroxide precipitate forms.

reactions.name: Carbon dioxide in limewater
reactions.notes: Excess CO2 can redissolve the precipitate.

reactions.name: Copper hydroxide precipitation from nitrate
reactions.conditions: Copper(II) nitrate solution with sodium hydroxide.

reactions.name: Ferric hydroxide precipitation from nitrate
reactions.conditions: Iron(III) nitrate solution with sodium hydroxide.

reactions.name: Ferrous hydroxide precipitation
reactions.conditions: Mix iron(II) sulfate and sodium hydroxide solutions.
reactions.notes: Iron(II) hydroxide can oxidize further in air.

reactions.name: Lead iodide precipitation
reactions.conditions: Mix lead nitrate and potassium iodide solutions.
reactions.observation: Bright yellow lead iodide precipitate forms.

reactions.name: Magnesium sulfate hardness removal
reactions.conditions: Add sodium carbonate to magnesium sulfate hard water.

reactions.name: Permanent hardness removal with washing soda
reactions.conditions: Add sodium carbonate to calcium-containing hard water.

reactions.name: Soap scum formation
reactions.conditions: Soap solution in calcium-containing hard water.
reactions.notes: Explains poor lathering in hard water.

reactions.name: Zinc hydroxide precipitation from nitrate
reactions.conditions: Zinc nitrate solution with sodium hydroxide.

reactions.name: Concentrated sulfuric acid oxidizes copper
reactions.observation: Copper sulfate, sulfur dioxide and water form.
reactions.notes: Shows oxidizing property of concentrated sulfuric acid.

reactions.name: Thermite reaction
reactions.conditions: Ignition of aluminum and iron oxide mixture.
reactions.observation: Very high heat; molten iron forms.

reactions.name: Ferric chloride formation
reactions.notes: A simple redox example using iron chlorides.

reactions.name: Sodium chloride redox synthesis
reactions.conditions: Sodium metal reacts with chlorine gas.
reactions.observation: Sodium loses electrons and chlorine gains electrons.
reactions.notes: Shows oxidation of sodium and reduction of chlorine.

reactions.name: Tollens silver mirror
reactions.observation: Silver mirror or grey silver deposit.

reactions.name: Daniell cell copper reduction
reactions.observation: Copper metal deposits on the cathode.
reactions.notes: Cathode half-reaction of the Daniell cell.

reactions.name: Silver electroplating cathode reaction
reactions.conditions: Object to be plated acts as cathode.
reactions.observation: Silver deposits as a thin coating.

reactions.name: Water electrolysis cathode reaction
reactions.conditions: Cathode during electrolysis of acidified water.

reactions.name: Haber ammonia synthesis
reactions.conditions: Iron catalyst, high pressure, controlled temperature.
reactions.observation: Ammonia forms reversibly; heat is released.

reactions.name: Hydrogen iodide reversible synthesis
reactions.observation: A reversible equilibrium forms hydrogen iodide.
reactions.notes: Useful classroom example of reversible reactions and equilibrium.

reactions.name: Roasting of cinnabar
reactions.observation: Mercury(II) oxide and sulfur dioxide form.
reactions.notes: Part of mercury extraction from cinnabar.

reactions.name: Roasting of galena
reactions.observation: Lead sulfide converts to lead oxide and sulfur dioxide.
reactions.notes: Sulfide ore roasting step before reduction.

reactions.name: Roasting of zinc sulfide
reactions.observation: Zinc sulfide converts to zinc oxide and sulfur dioxide.
reactions.notes: Prepares zinc oxide for carbon reduction.

reactions.name: Self-reduction of mercury oxide with cinnabar
reactions.observation: Mercury metal and sulfur dioxide form.

reactions.name: Iron displaces copper
reactions.conditions: Iron metal in copper(II) sulfate solution.
reactions.observation: Copper coats the iron; blue solution fades.
reactions.notes: Iron is more reactive than copper.

reactions.name: Magnesium with sulfuric acid
reactions.conditions: Magnesium metal and dilute sulfuric acid.

reactions.name: Zinc with hydrochloric acid
reactions.notes: Hydrogen pop test can confirm H2.

reactions.name: Zinc with sulfuric acid
reactions.conditions: Dilute sulfuric acid and zinc metal.
reactions.notes: Active metal displaces hydrogen from acid.

reactions.name: Blast furnace slag formation
reactions.notes: Removes silicon dioxide gangue from iron ore.

reactions.name: Chlorination of methane
reactions.notes: First step of methane substitution; further chlorination can give CH2Cl2, CHCl3 and CCl4.

reactions.name: Lauryl alcohol sulfation
reactions.conditions: Lauryl alcohol reacts with sulfuric acid.
reactions.notes: First step in sodium lauryl sulfate detergent production.

reactions.name: Thermal decomposition of calcium carbonate
reactions.notes: Endothermic decomposition used in lime manufacture.
